name: Aracine with Sardines and Saffron
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces salted sardines (rinsed, heads removed, and roughly chopped)
recipeInstructions:
FOR RICE: In a 4 to 6-quart saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil until smoking. Add half the chopped red onion and saffron and cook until softened, about 7 to 9 minutes. Add rice and chicken stock and bring to boil, uncovered. Cook until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender, about 25 minutes. Spread rice out on a large baking sheet, about 1/4-inch to 1/2-inch thick. When rice is cool, mix with Pecorino, egg yolks, and beaten eggs and set aside.
FOR STUFFING: In a 10- to 12-inch saute pan, cook remaining chopped onion in 2 tablespoons olive oil until light golden brown, about 10 minutes. Add tomato sauce, sardines, and mint and cook for 5 to 7 minutes until reduced by one third and quite thick. Set aside and allow to cool.
TO ASSEMBLE: Place flour in a shallow bowl, egg whites in another bowl, and bread crumbs in another bowl. Lightly beat the eggs with the yolks. To form aracine, place about 3 tablespoons of rice mixture in your palm and make an indentation with the back of a spoon. Spoon 2 tablespoons of sardine mixture into the indentation and form an open bowl out of the rice in your hand. Cover the filled portion with another tablespoon of rice mixture and use both hands to shape the rice into a ball. Continue with remaining rice mixture and stuffing until done. Roll each ball in flour, then egg whites, then bread crumbs and refrigerate for 1 hour, uncovered.
Heat oil in a fryer or deep frying pan to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Carefully place 3 balls at a time into the hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 3 to 4 minutes, stirring with tongs or a spoon to keep them moving. Drain on paper towels and place on a serving platter. Serve warm or at room temperature.
